criteria.label: Identity continuity
criteria.description: Same character remains clearly the same person across cuts (face, hair, body type, age).

criteria.label: Wardrobe/prop continuity
criteria.description: Wardrobe, key props, and held objects remain consistent unless the prompt explicitly changes them.

criteria.label: Dialogue attribution continuity
criteria.description: Spoken lines clearly belong to the visible speaker; avoid lines swapping to another mouth.

criteria.label: Lip-sync plausibility
criteria.description: Mouth motion plausibly matches speech timing and intensity; no obvious desync.

criteria.label: Temporal coherence
criteria.description: Cause/effect beats make sense; no teleporting objects/characters without justification.

criteria.label: Camera realism
criteria.description: Camera behavior looks physically plausible for the chosen style (phone footage + cinematic coverage).

fail_flags.label: FAIL: dialogue ownership splits across mouths
fail_flags.description: A single intended speaker's line appears to be spoken by multiple on-screen mouths, or switches mid-line.

fail_flags.label: FAIL: dialogue text not preserved
fail_flags.description: When dialogue is marked preserve/verbatim, the generated spoken dialogue materially diverges (wrong words, missing key phrase, swapped content, or unintelligible).

fail_flags.label: FAIL: dialogue/action mismatch
fail_flags.description: Spoken dialogue materially contradicts the visible actions/objects/events (e.g. describing throwing a ball while never throwing; naming the wrong task).

fail_flags.label: FAIL: dialogue line split across speakers
fail_flags.description: A single speaker-labeled line is split across multiple speakers (e.g. one character says the first half of another character’s line).

fail_flags.label: FAIL: role/action mismatch
fail_flags.description: A key action assigned to a specific character is performed by the wrong character, making roles ambiguous or inverted.

fail_flags.label: FAIL: physics plausibility break
fail_flags.description: Major physical plausibility failure: teleporting, phasing through solids, impossible object interactions (no contact/constraints), 'eating air', or tools actuating without being used.

fail_flags.label: FAIL: fourth-wall break
fail_flags.description: A character looks directly into the camera or performs to the viewer, breaking the intended observed/candid feel.

fail_flags.label: FAIL: audience misalignment
fail_flags.description: Dialogue/performance is delivered to the viewer/camera rather than between characters (e.g. speaking into camera instead of to each other).
